generated_code:
type Position = tuple[x: int, y: int]

enum EntityState:
    IDLE = 0
    MOVING = 1
    ATTACKING = 2

struct Velocity:
    dx: int
    dy: int

    def __init__(self, dx: int, dy: int):
        self.dx = dx
        self.dy = dy

@abstract
class Entity:
    position: Position
    state: EntityState

    def __init__(self, pos: Position):
        self.position = pos
        self.state = EntityState.IDLE

    @virtual
    def update(self, vel: Velocity) -> str:
        ...

    def get_location(self) -> str:
        px: int = self.position.x
        py: int = self.position.y
        return f"({px},{py})"

class Player(Entity):
    health: int

    def __init__(self, pos: Position, hp: int):
        super().__init__(pos)
        self.health = hp

    @override
    def update(self, vel: Velocity) -> str:
        old_x: int = self.position.x
        old_y: int = self.position.y
        new_x: int = old_x + vel.dx
        new_y: int = old_y + vel.dy

        if self.health > 50 and (vel.dx != 0 or vel.dy != 0):
            self.state = EntityState.MOVING
            self.position = (new_x, new_y)
            return f"player healthy move to {self.get_location()}"
        elif self.health > 0:
            self.state = EntityState.IDLE
            if vel.dx != 0 and vel.dy != 0:
                self.position = (new_x, new_y)
                return f"player weakened but moved to {self.get_location()}"
            else:
                return "player weakened, staying put"
        else:
            return "player defeated"

class Enemy(Entity):
    aggression: int

    def __init__(self, pos: Position, aggro: int):
        super().__init__(pos)
        self.aggression = aggro

    @override
    def update(self, vel: Velocity) -> str:
        dist: int = abs(vel.dx) + abs(vel.dy)

        if self.aggression >= dist and not (vel.dx == 0 and vel.dy == 0):
            self.state = EntityState.ATTACKING
            return f"enemy attacking from {self.get_location()}"
        elif dist > 0:
            old_x: int = self.position.x
            old_y: int = self.position.y
            self.position = (old_x + vel.dx // 2, old_y + vel.dy // 2)
            self.state = EntityState.MOVING
            return f"enemy pursuing to {self.get_location()}"
        else:
            return "enemy waiting"

def simulate(entities: list[Entity], velocities: list[Velocity]) -> None:
    i: int = 0
    while i < len(entities):
        vel: Velocity = velocities[i] if i < len(velocities) else Velocity(0, 0)
        result: str = entities[i].update(vel)
        print(result)
        i = i + 1

def main():
    player: Player = Player((10, 10), 75)
    weak_player: Player = Player((5, 5), 25)
    defeated: Player = Player((0, 0), 0)
    enemy: Enemy = Enemy((20, 20), 10)
    passive: Enemy = Enemy((1, 1), 0)

    print(player.update(Velocity(2, 2)))
    print(weak_player.update(Velocity(3, 4)))
    print(defeated.update(Velocity(1, 1)))
    print(enemy.update(Velocity(5, 5)))
    print(enemy.update(Velocity(0, 0)))
    print(passive.update(Velocity(2, 0)))
expected_output:
player healthy move to (12,12)
player weakened but moved to (8,9)
player defeated
enemy attacking from (20,20)
enemy waiting
enemy waiting
actual_output:
player healthy move to (12,12)
player weakened but moved to (8,9)
player defeated
enemy attacking from (20,20)
enemy waiting
enemy pursuing to (2,1)
